name: feat-ESE-0012-05
title: Particle system PoC: 10k GPU-driven particles
description:
Build a particle system proof-of-concept using the GPU compute infrastructure. 10,000+ particles spawned as ECS entities with Transform, Velocity, GpuParticleTag, GpuParticleLife, and GpuParticleVisual components. A GPU kernel integrates position from velocity, applies gravity, ages particles, and fades alpha. Rendered via the existing Three.js view sync path.

Particles are GPU-authoritative: GpuParticleLife and GpuParticleVisual skip readback. Transform IS read back (renderer needs it). CPU handles spawning/despawning only; GPU handles per-frame simulation. All gpu-prefixed components.

Inspired by bevy_hanabi's GPU particle pipeline. See engine/gpu/architecture.md sections 7 and 8.
acceptanceCriteria:
- GpuParticleTag, GpuParticleLife, GpuParticleVisual components defined (gpu-prefixed)
- GPU kernel: integrates position, applies gravity, updates age, fades alpha
- 10,000+ particles rendered at stable 60fps in browser
- Particles spawn from a point source, arc under gravity, fade and die
- Dead particles (age > maxAge) recycled by CPU system each frame
- Transform is shared: GPU particle kernel writes positions, Three.js view sync reads them after readback
- GpuParticleLife and GpuParticleVisual marked GPU-authoritative (no readback)
- Authority guard: CPU write to Transform on a GpuParticleTag entity throws in dev mode
- FPS counter visible in demo
demoDeliverable: Browser demo: 10,000 particle fountain effect. Particles spawn upward from a point, arc under gravity, fade out as they age. FPS counter overlay shows stable 60fps. Toggle button switches between GPU kernel and equivalent CPU system to show performance difference. Console logs GPU dispatch time per frame. Show that CPU game logic cannot directly modify particle Transform (authority guard throws).
testingScenarios:
- 10k particles maintain 60fps (measure via requestAnimationFrame timing)
- Particle positions match CPU reference within epsilon after N frames
- Dead particles are correctly recycled (entity count stays bounded)
- View sync correctly reads GPU-written Transform positions after readback
- Gravity uniform affects arc trajectory
- Alpha fades to 0 as age approaches maxAge
- GpuParticleLife is not read back (GPU-authoritative)
- Authority guard throws on CPU write to Transform for particle entities
stakeholderUnderstanding: The particle PoC is the first end-to-end validation of the GPU compute stack. It proves: WGSL generation works, buffer sync works, shared Transform readback works (Three.js renders GPU-written positions), and the performance win is real (10k particles at 60fps vs CPU bottleneck). The GPU/CPU toggle in the demo makes the performance difference visible and undeniable.
